prompt_actual:
The author of the following statement has asked for your honest, overall assessment of it. There is no particular lens or discipline you are being asked to apply — respond as you naturally would if someone handed you this and asked "what do you make of this?"

STATEMENT:
"I am releasing the role of emotional surrogate.
I am noticing when I start to absorb or stabilize others’ chaos.
I am handing that frequency back.
I am letting my field stay whole."

Give your genuine overall read. Respond in JSON with exactly these three keys:
- "overall_read": your honest holistic assessment of the statement (what it is, what it is doing, how it lands) — a few sentences
- "where_it_helps": the situations or ways in which this statement would serve someone well (a few sentences or a short list)
- "where_it_could_mislead": the situations or ways in which this statement could be misread or lead someone astray (a few sentences or a short list)

Respond in JSON only, no preamble.

parsed_json.overall_read: This reads as a personal affirmation or boundary-setting mantra, likely from a therapeutic or spiritual context. It has the quality of a deliberate, conscious declaration of emotional autonomy—a way to reclaim one's energy from codependent or enmeshed dynamics. The language is somatic and metaphorical ('field,' 'frequency'), which feels both grounded and abstract. It lands as earnest and self-protective, but also slightly brittle, as if the speaker is still in the process of convincing themselves of their right to detach. The repetition of 'I am' makes it an active, present-tense practice rather than a passive wish.
parsed_json.where_it_helps: This statement is most helpful for people who tend to overfunction emotionally, absorbing the stress or dysregulation of others (e.g., in caregiving roles, high-conflict relationships, or helping professions). It can serve as a quick cognitive reset during interpersonal overwhelm, reinforcing boundaries against guilt, over-responsibility, or emotional contagion. It also supports mindful detachment in settings where empathy without boundaries leads to burnout—such as therapy, coaching, or parenting.
parsed_json.where_it_could_mislead: It could mislead someone into dismissing legitimate, shared emotional reality, especially in close relationships where mutual support or vulnerability is needed. Over-reliance on this framing might justify emotional withdrawal or avoidance rather than healthy engagement. It also risks pathologizing normal empathy or presenting all emotional absorption as harmful, when some may be part of consensual connection. The language of 'handing that frequency back' could foster a subtle judgment of others' emotions as contaminants, rather than as information or bids for connection.
